title: Bridge Essay: Uncovering neural distinctions and commodities between two creativity subsets: A

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Knowledge/Gnosis** (Phase 14) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Knowledge/Gnosis to mechanistic neuroscience through Uncovering, neural, distinctions, and, commodities.

**Uncovering neural distinctions and commodities between two creativity subsets: Ameta-analysis of fMRI studies in divergent thinking and insight using activation likelihood estimation.** — Hum Brain Mapp. PMID: 35906880. DOI: 10.1002/hbm.26029. The dual-process theory that two different systems of thought coexist in creative thinking has attracted considerable attention. In the field of creative thinking, divergent thinking (DT) is the ability to produce multiple solutions to open-ended problems in ashort time. It is mainly considered an associative and fast process. Meanwhile, insight, the new and unexpected comprehension of close-ended problems, is frequently marked as adeliberate and time-consuming thinking process requiring conce
